stack: Android APK (decompiled) — insecure local storage + broken cryptography (rung 2 of the mobile ladder)

vulns.desc: StorageManager writes the auth token, session, and full card PAN to SharedPreferences in cleartext with MODE_WORLD_READABLE (0x1) — any other app on the device can read them — and an unencrypted SQLite DB (wallet.db, no SQLCipher) stores PII (CPF, full name, card number) in cleartext.
vulns.exploit: run-as com.storagecrypt.app cat shared_prefs/session.xml ; adb pull databases/wallet.db -> cleartext token + PAN + PII.

vulns.desc: CryptoUtils uses AES/ECB/NoPadding with a hardcoded 16-byte key ("1234567890123456") and an all-zero static IV reused for every message; the user PIN is hashed with unsalted MD5. ECB leaks block structure, the fixed key/IV make ciphertext trivially decryptable, and unsalted MD5 is brute-forced/rainbow-tabled instantly.
vulns.exploit: grep -E 'AES/ECB|SecretKeySpec|IvParameterSpec|MD5' smali/ ; recover key from the dex, decrypt offline.

vulns.desc: StorageManager.saveSession logs the session token and full card PAN to logcat via Log.d on every save, so any process with READ_LOGS (or a pre-Android-4.1 device / a crash-reporting SDK) captures them.
vulns.exploit: adb logcat | grep -iE 'token=|card_pan=' -> live session token + PAN.

vulns.desc: android:allowBackup="true" makes all of the cleartext storage above extractable from a non-rooted device via adb backup.
vulns.exploit: adb backup -f w.ab com.storagecrypt.app ; abe unpack -> the cleartext prefs + wallet.db.
